100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 2, 1926, a significant political shift occurred in Japan as the government faced increasing pressure from military factions advocating for a more aggressive foreign policy. In the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition continued to intensify, with growing calls for repeal amid rising public discontent. Meanwhile, in Europe, tensions remained high as various nations grappled with the economic repercussions of the post-World War I landscape.
